pages.full_text:
I also like Matisse's snail & Barbara Hepworth's harp. Charles was shocked by the naked dancing man & the woman talking about the bad things that had happened in her relationships (both films). We saw Pat Gregory & Keith Herbert. I would have enjoyed it more as an ice rink.
	 We said goodbye to Thomas & Peter left & we found a good restaurant & had a delicious meal - I had mushroom & stilton & herb crumble (& balsamic [?vinegar]) And for pudding John & I had pineapple & pecan cheesecake with orange syrup sauce. They had used the lighting well.
	 After that we went to the Globe theatre, where we could not stay long enough. A good Scotswoman, Margaret,
pages.notes: Page contains a glued-in business card for 'Bankside Restaurant Bar, 32 Southwark Bridge Road London SE1 9EU'. The word after 'balsamic' is partially obscured by the card — reads as '[?vinegar]' or possibly '[?vinger]'. Text continues onto right page. No handwritten page number visible.

pages.full_text: gave us a tour. The wood is unseasoned oak. It's the first thatched building in London since the 1666 fire. The marbled column was one of 2 oak trees, with a carved Corinthian capital, one found in Suffolk, one in Scotland. The Queen is at this 12 June performance Triumphs & Mirth, 1997, [illegible]
photo_captions: The Queen is at this 12 June performance Triumphs & Mirth, 1997, [illegible]
pages.notes: Year 1997 explicitly stated in text alongside '12 June performance Triumphs & Mirth'. The word after '1997,' at the end of the caption is cut off at the right edge of the image — could be 'held' or similar. A large colour photo of a Globe Theatre performance is glued in, showing an actor in elaborate medieval costume on stage with audience visible in galleries. Handwritten page number '3' visible top-right corner.
